item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, we never got that addendum unexpunged, nor did we get that tale either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit: I upvoted because this is cool and it's well done. The expungement in Addendum A annoys me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If older cameras don't catch these young rascals, couldn't older cameras be used for actual security and newer cameras for seeing these criminals at large? Or, the foundation could use these two types of cameras to verify that something anomalous is going down when one camera's feed begins to differ from another, reducing the amount of false-positives. Or, I could be overthinking this.

Anyway, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, Series II is just chock-full of good media skips I never read until now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the expungement, though that won't stop me from upvoting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one and I think the idea was executed well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like this

[DATA EXPUNGED]10-a

I know it's Pooryoric's thing but that doesn't prevent me from disliking it.

Upvoted however

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On █/█/19██, a teller was shown in SCP-1110 footage to resist, and was subsequently shot by SCP-1110-2. Post incident reviews with other bank employees revealed [DATA EXPUNGED].

OH COME ON.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could scribble up an AWCY tale for you to look at and maybe work on if you want. I chose AWCY because it seems that MC&D or the CI have more reliable ways of amassing larger sums of money… Plus this seems like more AWCY's unorthodox style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what would happen if a patron in the video fought back, attacking/killing one or both of the robbers. Or what comes about if the police DO show to the semi-false bank robbery. Would they appear in the video, and arrest them? Get into a shootout? So much can be done with this, like many SCPs, and it saddens me that the true depth of the idea will never be explored. In short, I want most SCPs to get their own movie/book deals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Video-only bank robbers is just one of those ideas that click with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My original plan was actually to have them funding some GOI or another, but the Tale never happpened. I'd love it if someone else were to run with the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd love it if these things were agents of the Chaos Insurgency or MC&D or something like that, maybe even AWCY. After all, crazy mothas need money too right? And who's to say they haven't got an object that lets them phase into video-world.
That said, I usually don't get butthurt about redactions like most people on this site seem to, but these just annoyed me for some reason. So you don't get -1, but neither +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My theory: They're bank robbers in a parallel dimension who have found out how to swap the video feeds between dimensions. And swap the money from our dimension with the money in theirs.

It's a method to avoid conviction. No evidence of their crimes, no actual money stolen. In their dimension, it's stage magic and performance art in public.

I suppose they could be arrested for being a public nuisance… But, yeah, what if the robbers have to murerlize someone? Do the bodies get switched too, like the money?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everytime I read this, I upvote it.
But then I can't because I've already upvoted it before.
And then I'm sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No one knows who or what they are. But one thing is certain: they need more money. I like this idea, because it's bizarre and strange but not actively dangerous. The Foundation only has to cover the occasional bank robbery, which by most containment standards is a pretty good deal.

But the again… What if something goes wrong and this duo takes hostages?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alternate universe bleeding over into ours? Were there any robbers matching the descriptions who were killed or arrested early in their career?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not much in the way of threatening of creepy, but the sheer double-yoo-tee-effitude of this one makes me like it.

Upvoted and waiting to see it expanded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are probably fronts that are know by both sides, so they can make contacts as needed.

Also, if someone lets something slip and they hear "SCP" it would be better to be able to say "Oh, I mean the 'Skeptics of Conspiracy and the Paranormal' Society! They check this weird stuff out all the time. You should expect a call from them…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always favored the more illusive and harder to contain SCPs like this one.
the whole interfering with the law goes right up in Clef's whole report on "Foundation Evils".
So I'll give it the benefit of a doubt and give it +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, though you could cut down on some unneeded description.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope. The style is exactly how I want it.

EDIT: Saw your PM. PM me revision suggestions and I'll read over 'em, you have a point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With your permission, I propose to undertake an aggressive edit for style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
NOTE: For small issues such as spelling, grammar, and capitalization errors, it's perfectly acceptable to simply edit the page and make corrections yourself, so long as you are not altering the actual content of the article.

It's less acceptable to be passive aggressive about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can the original author capitalize all his Gammas, please?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's right.

Besides, MC&D doesn't loot. They have people for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, what you are forgetting is that there's plenty of SCP companies, (like Slovenske Celulozky a Papierne) that have nothing to do with the Foundation. And because the name isn't restricted, you'd get a fuckton of false positives on the supposed looting, and that's all kinds of expensive (such as bribing the police to put it under carpet)
Furthermore, going into a Foundation frontend, where the personnel is likely armed isn't that trivial either. They found it cheaper to rely on corruption or extortion of personnel, basically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't have to, really. In fact, it's likely most don't. I just think the acronym is fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you asked! I'm willing to bet that whatever answer you come up with will be better than the one I did, which is why I didn't include it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a random unrelated thought, but why do Foundation fronts have to carry the oh-so-obvious SCP abbreviation? If you were setting up an obvious business front for the SCP, what's stopping MC&D from just barging in and looting the whole damn place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Following the manifestation, the patron commented to Foundation interviewers that his watch was present, but had stopped at the instant of removal shown in the video.

So it steals physical cash, but the "spirit" or "essence" of other things? Wouldn't it be more consistent for the watch to just vanish of the patron's wrist?

Or is it that whatever is stolen stops working, but the only way for cash to stop working is to not exist anymore?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It will be unexpunged later, but I'm still working on a Foundation tale involving the expunged bit, and don't want to spoilers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had this thought. The redactions by themselves kept me from upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm.

I nearly upvoted based on the interesting concept alone, but… there's not enough here to really interest me. In light of that, Addendum-1110-a actively annoys me. From a writing standpoint, that seems to me to be badly used expungement.

So, ultimately, I feel neutral about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, uh, thanks.

So, yeh, new SCP up. Come read it, my chiiiillldren!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
formatting fuckery makes me angry

so i fixed it for you
